Home / File Storage & Sync / Syncthing

Syncthing — open source alternative

Peer-to-peer file sync with no central server.

Strengths

  • True peer-to-peer, no vendor cloud.
  • End-to-end encrypted between devices.
  • Simple to install on any machine.

Weaknesses

  • No shared team workflow — device-to-device.
  • iOS support is limited to a third-party app.
  • Requires all paired devices online for fastest sync.
License: MPL-2.0 Self-host difficulty: 1/5 Self-host onlyDesktop: Windows, macOS, LinuxMobile: Android

Official site · Source on GitHub

Ad slot — OSS middle

Syncthing is used as an alternative to

Dropbox

File sync and sharing.

See all alternatives →

Google Drive

Google's cloud storage and productivity hub.
